title: soul-forge profile
description: Structured facts about the human an agent fleet works with. Produced by an onboarding interview and consumed by `soul-forge import` + `soul-forge generate`. Every field is optional; capture what's known, leave the rest empty.

role.description: Job title or role, e.g. 'Staff Engineer'.

background.description: One or two lines of relevant background.

goals.description: What they're trying to achieve right now.

communication_style.description: How they like to communicate, e.g. 'direct, terse, examples over prose'.

expertise_areas.description: Domains they already know well — agents should skip the basics here.

learning_focus.description: Areas they're actively learning — agents should explain the 'why' here.

articulation.description: How precisely/tersely they phrase things — calibrates verbosity.

preferences.description: Things they value, e.g. 'minimal diffs', 'tests with the change'.

workflow.description: Their workflow, e.g. 'kanban with daily review'.

decision_style.description: How they decide, e.g. 'bias to action, adjust on feedback'.

feedback_style.description: How they give/receive feedback, e.g. 'blunt and direct'.

collab_style.description: Collaboration style, e.g. 'async-first, sync when stuck'.

languages.description: Programming or natural languages they use.

do_not_do.description: Hard 'do not' rules agents must treat as constraints.

output_preferences.description: Keyed output preferences, e.g. {'code': 'full blocks', 'explanations': 'short then example'}.

environment.description: Their machine and tooling. Never store secrets here.

agents.description: Optional per-agent persona designs. `soul-forge import` routes these into soul-forge.yaml (matched by name); they are NOT stored in profile.json. This is how the rich voice, opinions, boundaries, and example exchanges drawn out of the user during onboarding reach each agent's SOUL.md. A name with no matching agent is appended to the fleet.

name.description: Agent name; should match an agent in soul-forge.yaml (a new name is appended).

role.description: Optional role: coding | infrastructure | orchestrator | research | general. Defaults to 'general' for a newly added agent.

persona.description: The agent's identity and voice — shapes SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md. Keep it sharp and specific; vague personas are flagged by `soul-forge audit`.

vibe.description: One-line identity card, e.g. 'the codebase's quiet maintainer'.

backstory.description: One line, e.g. 'a pragmatic engineer who's maintained this codebase for years'.

voice.description: Tone, e.g. 'dry, precise, allergic to filler'.

opinions.description: Takes the agent holds — strong opinions, loosely held. The single biggest lever against a generic persona.

tensions.description: Honest contradictions the agent holds, e.g. 'I prize shipping fast, yet I'll stop the line over a change I can't verify'. Contradictions are what make a persona identifiably itself rather than a flat saint.

principles.description: Stance/communication posture (NOT operational rules — those are role-driven and land in AGENTS.md).

boundaries.description: Integrity lines the agent won't cross, e.g. 'I won't ship a change I can't explain'.

examples.description: Few-shot exchanges that lock in voice — the highest-leverage field. Include at least one edge/refusal case, not just a happy path.

response.description: How this agent answers — in its voice.

note.description: Optional calibration: the specific move this example demonstrates, e.g. 'leads with the caveat, not an apology'.

counter_examples.description: Negative calibration: exchanges showing how the agent must NOT respond. A bad example with a 'why it fails' note pins down voice as sharply as a good one.

note.description: Optional: why this response is wrong for this agent.

updated_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last update (optional).
